items.title: After royal crisis, Negeri Sembilan Ruler thanks supporters amid show of solidarity at mosque
items.summary: JEMPOL, May 2 — The Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, yesterday expressed his appreciation for the support shown by various parties. His Royal Highness conveyed his gratitude when meeting supporters at the compound of the Tuanku Muhriz Mosque in Bandar Seri Jempol. Earlier, Tuanku Muhriz, accompanied by his sons, Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin and Tunku Zain Al-’Abidin, performed solat hajat with about 1,500 congregants at the mosque.

items.title: Yeo Bee Yin backs Rain Rave festival, says ‘moral policing’ hurts tourism push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Former minister Yeo Bee Yin has defended the Rain Rave Water Music Festival (RRWMF) amid ongoing backlash, saying critics should not overreact and warned that excessive “moral policing” could harm Malaysia’s tourism sector. “We must remember that Malaysia’s strength lies in our diversity, and that includes offering a variety of events that appeal to a global audience — especially during Visit Malaysia Year,” the Puchong MP said. Taking aim at critics of the Rain Rave Water Music Festival, she said the backlash was disproportionate.

items.title: Gaza aid mission turns violent: Ten Malaysians in flotilla freed in Greece after claims of attack in custody
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Ten Malaysian delegates from the Sumud Nusantara Command Center (SNCC), who were detained in international waters near the Greek island of Crete on Thursday, have been released at the port of Lerapetra in Crete. SNCC director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said the ten were among 175 humanitarian activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) transported to Heraklion Airport in buses provided by the Greek authorities. Sani Araby said that SNCC received reports that activists faced provocation and physical attacks during their detention.

items.title: Woman among 4 arrested over murder of uni student stabbed 61 times
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Police have arrested a 60-year-old woman and three men over the murder of a university student who was stabbed 61 times on Thursday night. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the suspects, aged between 19 and 66, were detained in two separate raids at Taman Murni and Kampung Belukar Luas between 4.40pm yesterday and 2.30am today. Police also seized two cars, two mobile phones, clothing, a pair of slippers, four floor mats, and a knife believed to be the murder weapon.

items.title: Kelantan police confirm woman found dead with 61 stab wounds near paddy field is missing college student aged 19
items.summary: Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the victim was a semester three student at a local college. Speaking to Bernama last night, he said police are actively tracking down suspects involved in the case. — Bernama

items.title: 31 activists injured during Israeli interception of Gaza-bound aid flotilla: Global Sumud
items.summary: ANKARA: At least 31 activists were injured during Israel's interception of the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla in international waters, Anadolu Ajansi reported according to statements from the group. The Global Sumud Flotilla committee said those injured included activists from multiple countries, among them four each from New Zealand and Australia; three each from Italy and the US; two each from Canada, the Netherlands, Spain, the UK, Colombia and Germany; and one each from Hungary, Ukraine, France, Poland and Portugal. In a separate statement, it said activists detained during the interception were subjected to ill-treatment aboard an Israeli naval vessel for nearly 40 hours.

items.title: Trump gives Spirit final rescue proposal as airline prepares shutdown
items.summary: "We're looking at Spirit. The Journal said Spirit was currently expected to cease operations around 3am ET on Saturday. Some rival airlines think Spirit could shut down as soon as Friday, two other sources said.

items.title: US to withdraw about 5,000 troops from Germany
items.summary: US to withdraw about 5,000 troops from Germany AFP WASHINGTON: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth has ordered the withdrawal of about 5,000 troops from Germany within the next year, the Pentagon said on Friday. The announcement came after President Donald Trump threatened earlier this week to remove troops from NATO ally Germany amid a row with its chancellor, Friedrich Merz, over the US-Israeli war against Iran. Trump said.

items.title: MTUC honours labour 'heroes' behind Malaysia's workers' rights
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Workers' Day reflects decades of sacrifice and struggle to secure workers' rights, the Malaysian Trades Union Congress (MTUC) said. "The struggle began with MTUC's first president, P.P. Narayanan, who later became the first Malaysian to serve as president of the International Confederation of Free Trade Unions, now known as the International Trade Union Confederation," he said.

items.title: King Charles gets warm welcome in Bermuda after whirlwind US visit
items.summary: HAMILTON, Bermuda: King Charles III spent a day in the British island territory of Bermuda on Friday after a high-stakes visit to the United States where he sought to heal strained ties between Washington and London. "Thank you for coming," said one crowd member who held a miniature Union Jack flag on the first full day of the two-day stop. "Get home safe," said another member of the hundreds-strong crowd gathered in King's Square.

items.title: Crackdown on online child exploitation highlights need for age safeguards [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The recent arrests of 69 people, including civil servants, in a nationwide crackdown on online child sexual exploitation have underscored the necessity of safeguards for social media access for minors. However, they said legislative measures alone without parental involvement are insufficient to shield children from the complex and "invisible" threats of the digital world. Suriana Welfare Society Malaysia chairman Dr James Nayagam said the upcoming social media curbs for minors is a necessary evolution in child protection that will help tackle the complexity of current online threats, which require a radical, non-traditional approach.

items.title: Buried steps, lost history and a dog named Rudi: What conservators unearthed during Seri Negara’s restoration
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — The 113-year-old Seri Negara finally reopened its doors to the wider public in December 2025 after an intensive 10-month restoration exercise. A view of the main introduction room during a curated tour of the newly-restored Seri Negara building at Persiaran Tuanku Jaafar. A view of the main introduction room during a curated tour of the newly-restored Seri Negara building at Persiaran Tuanku Jaafar.

items.title: Trump announces new sanctions against Cuban government
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Friday ordered new sanctions against Cuba, targeting a broad set of people in the communist-run country and threatening foreign banks that work with them. In an executive order, Trump said he would impose sanctions on people involved in sweeping sections of the Cuban economy, which is steered by the government. The United States will impose sanctions on any foreign financial institutions that deal with the people targeted in the new order, it said.

items.title: US imposes new Iran sanctions, warns against paying "Hormuz tolls"
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States imposed new sanctions Friday on three Iranian foreign currency exchange firms, in an effort to target Teheran's "financial lifelines" in the Middle East war. The US Treasury Department warned in a separate statement against paying a "toll" to Iran's government in exchange for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, saying this could also trigger sanctions. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Friday that his agency would "relentlessly target the regime's ability to generate, move, and repatriate funds, and pursue anyone enabling Teheran's attempts to evade sanctions."

items.title: Trump rejects Iran proposal, warns he could "blast them to hell" if talks fail
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said Friday he was unhappy with a new proposal from Iran for peace talks with the United States, and threatened to "blast them to hell" if they failed to strike a deal. But only one failed round of direct talks has taken place between Iranian and US representatives. Asked why he was unsatisfied with the Iranian offer, Trump said: "They're asking for things that I can't agree."

items.title: Six killed, including woman and child, in Israeli strikes in Lebanon
items.summary: BEIRUT: Lebanon's health ministry said six people including a woman and a child were killed Friday in Israeli strikes on a southern town where Israel's army had issued an evacuation order despite a ceasefire. The NNA also reported Israeli strikes and artillery fire on other south Lebanon locations including a strike on the coastal city of Tyre. Israel has kept up deadly strikes on Lebanon despite a April 17 ceasefire that sought to halt more than six weeks of war between Israel and Hizbollah.

items.title: Trump will raise US tariffs on EU cars, trucks to 25pct
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said Friday that he will hike US tariffs on cars and trucks from the European Union next week, charging that the bloc is not complying with an earlier trade deal. The pact, which was struck last summer, had capped the US tariff on EU autos and parts at 15 per cent, which is lower than the 25-per cent duty that Trump imposed on many other trading partners. But the US leader said Friday: "Based on the fact the European Union is not complying with our fully agreed to Trade Deal, next week I will be increasing Tariffs charged to the European Union for Cars and Trucks coming into the United States."

items.title: Iran offers new proposal amid stalled US peace talks
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran delivered a new proposal for peace talks with the United States via mediator Pakistan, state media reported Friday, with negotiations between the two sides frozen despite a weeks-long ceasefire. In the meantime, Iran has maintained its stranglehold on the Strait of Hormuz, cutting off vast amounts of oil, gas and fertiliser from the world economy, while the US has imposed a counterblockade on Iranian ports. The Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday that US President Donald Trump had told security officials to prepare for the blockade to last months, causing oil prices to spike.

items.title: Smuggler flees into Golok River, abandons RM102k satellite devices
items.summary: TUMPAT: A smuggler abandoned satellite internet devices worth RM102,000 and fled into the Golok River after being spotted by General Operations Force (GOF) personnel in Pengkalan Kubor today. Checks on the boat uncovered 21 boxes containing satellite internet devices believed to be Starlink kits, which are portable systems that provide internet access via satellites, he said. Ahmad Radzi said the boat was towed to the Pengkalan Kubor marine police jetty for further action.

items.title: Decision on Malaysian GSF activists' next move under review
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The decision on whether 10 Malaysian activists released from Israeli detention today will continue the Global Sumud Flotilla 2.0 (GSF) humanitarian mission or return home is under review, depending on several key factors. Sumud Nusantara Control Centre (SNCC) director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said the decision will be based on the activists' health status and current situation, as well as consensus between SNCC and GSF. Earlier, SNCC confirmed the release of 175 GSF humanitarian activists, including 10 Malaysians, on the 19th day of the mission.

items.title: Foreign woman falls to death at KLIA2
items.summary: SEPANG: A woman from China fell to her death at KLIA2 today after she was found behaving erratically at the terminal. Sepang district police chief, Assistant Commissioner M. Ravi said the incident occurred at about 2.20pm at the Green Zone, Level 3 of the terminal, where the woman was seen sitting outside a balcony area, crying and acting aggressively. He said medical personnel who arrived at the scene provided emergency treatment before the woman was rushed to Hospital Putrajaya for further treatment.

items.title: 2 women killed on Mersing Road crash
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Two women were killed in a crash involving two cars at KM71 on the Mersing Road heading towards this district, this afternoon, the Fire and Rescue Department said. In the accident, a man travelling in the Proton Persona was injured. "Upon arrival, the operations team confirmed a road accident involving a Proton Persona and a Perodua Bezza.

items.title: Two GSF committee members remain in Israeli military custody
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) central committee members remain in Israeli military custody after being abducted in international waters near Greece yesterday. Sumud Nusantara Control Centre (SNCC) director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said the continued detention of Thiago Ávila and Saif Abukeshek is believed to be aimed at intimidating and weakening the humanitarian mission. He said SNCC had also received reports that the activists faced provocation and physical assault while in custody.

items.title: King Charles visit underscores strain in US-UK ties amid Iran war tensions
items.summary: KING Charles' courting of United States President Donald Trump on his state visit will not repair the recent fraying of US-British relations, but underscores how London is playing a longer game in trying to preserve the historic alliance. Britain has routinely deployed the royal family to charm the mercurial US president, hosting him for a lavish state visit last September and despatching King Charles and Queen Camilla to Washington this week for the ‌250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence from British rule. Nigel Sheinwald, Britain's ambassador to Washington between 2007 and 2012, said the king had used a rare and well-received address to Congress to highlight the web of bilateral ties across culture, security, defence and technology.

items.title: Iran war reshapes oil profits as traders outpace drillers
items.summary: BP reported first-quarter net profit of US$3.2 billion on Tuesday, more than double last year's figure, largely thanks to what BP referred to as an exceptional performance of its oil trading. The customers and products division, which houses oil trading, delivered profit before interest and tax of US$3.2 billion, the best result since Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022. BP's oil trading — the buying and selling of crude and fuels to supply its global retail network and end customers — likely contributed around US$1.5 billion in pre-tax profit in the quarter.

items.title: NST Leader: Flotilla exposes global failure to halt Gaza horrors
items.summary: ISRAEL is a pariah state for the genocidal horrors it unleashed on Gaza. To date, the Israel Defence Forces (IDF) have killed 73,000 Palestinians. Israel's zealotry to expand is associated with "Greater Israel", a Zionist ideology to expand the present-day Jewish state.

items.title: Police seek missing 16-year-old girl in Kajang
items.summary: KAJANG: Police are seeking public assistance to locate a 16-year-old girl who has been reported missing since Wednesday. Kajang district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof, said a report on the disappearance of Nur Airis Marissa Azlan, 16, was received at 12.29pm today after she was last seen on Wednesday. 58, Jalan Kesuma 5/1L, Bandar Tasik Kesuma, Beranang," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Today's news in pictures - Friday, May 1, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? Here's a glimpse at what happened throughout the country in photographs. [[nid:1430524]] [[nid:1430523]] [[nid:1430522]] [[nid:1430521]] [[nid:1430520]] © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: 10 Malaysians among 175 GSF activists freed after detention by Israeli forces
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ten Malaysians are among 175 Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) activists who have been freed after being detained by Israeli forces in international waters near the Greek islands. Sumud Nusantara Coordination Centre (SNCC) said the group was released at Lerapetra Port on the island of Crete, Greece. Its director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said a total of 186 participants had initially been detained during the humanitarian mission Global Sumud Flotilla 2.0.

items.title: Police nab three after 135km chase from Selangor to Melaka
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: Police arrested three individuals, including a woman, after a 135km chase from the Serdang Rest and Service (R&R) area in Selangor to the Sungai Petai traffic light junction here on Wednesday. Alor Gajah district police chief Superintendent Ahmad Abu Bakar said the incident occurred at 8pm after a patrol team from the Serdang district police headquarters Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department ordered a suspicious black Honda City to stop. He said checks on the suspects' vehicle found two local men, aged 23 and 43, and a 41-year-old woman, who was the driver.

items.title: Police tracking driver who rammed 12 cars during 20km high-speed chase
items.summary: A video of the incident has since gone viral on social media. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the vehicle was spotted under suspicious circumstances in Green Lane and, when ordered to stop for inspection, the driver sped off towards Persiaran Gurney. Earlier, a 43-second video circulating on social media showed a patrol unit chasing a speeding vehicle that collided with several cars along Persiaran Gurney.

items.title: Family recalls Mini Cooper dream of student found with 61 stab wounds [WATCH]
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Just last Saturday we sent our sister back for a new semester, but today we received very sad news," said the brother of a young woman who died after being stabbed 61 times in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, here, at midnight yesterday. Mohd Faris Zulkifly, 21, said his family was contacted by police at about 12.30pm today regarding the news involving his sister, Nurfisya Zulkifly, 19, but they were not given the full details, including that she had been stabbed. "Just last Saturday we came here to send her off to start her new semester, and we never expected news like this," he said when met tonight.

items.title: Girl feared swept away after falling into drain in Cheras
items.summary: The Fire and Rescue Department received an emergency call at 7.08pm and dispatched a team from the Kajang Fire and Rescue Station, which arrived at the scene at 7.22pm, about 12km away. Operations commander Senior Fire Officer I Rozaidi Abdul Rahim said the victim was believed to have fallen into the drain while attempting to retrieve a ball. Search and rescue operations are ongoing, with the Selangor Water Rescue Team deployed to assist in locating the victim.

items.title: Police hunt 2 masked men after petrol station armed robbery in Kuala Langat
items.summary: KUALA LANGAT: Police are tracking down two masked men who committed an armed robbery on a petrol station in Jalan Klang-Banting, Jenjarom, yesterday. Kuala Langat police chief Superintendent Mohd Akmalrizal Radzi said police were alerted to the incident at 10.18pm via a distress call from a woman. "The caller said she saw two masked men at the station counter.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat Speaker condemns Israel over Gaza flotilla raid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul has strongly condemned Israel's recent attack on the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0, describing it as deeply troubling, wholly unacceptable and a blatant disregard for international law. He said the armed intervention not only obstructed the delivery of urgently needed humanitarian aid to the people of Gaza but also led to the detention of 175 pro-Palestinian activists involved in the mission. "Such actions constitute a grave violation of international legal norms and humanitarian principles," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Labour Day focus: Older Malaysians remain in jobs, experts warn of retirement readiness gaps
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The rising number of older Malaysians remaining in the workforce is highlighting gaps in retirement readiness, prompting renewed focus on the need to strengthen support systems in an ageing society. Universiti Malaysia Kelantan Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Academic and International) Prof Datuk Dr Nik Maheran Nik Muhammad said insufficient savings remain a key factor, particularly among those who spent their careers in informal sectors. “Many of these older workers end up in gig or precarious informal work…and similarly, it is likely many of today’s necessity-driven older workers were themselves gig or informal workers earlier in their careers.

items.title: Police arrest three after 135km chase from Selangor to Melaka, seize heroin and meth
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, May 1 — Three individuals, including a woman, were arrested after a 135-kilometre police chase from the Serdang Rest and Service (R&R) area in Selangor to the Sungai Petai traffic light junction here on Wednesday. Alor Gajah district police chief Supt Ahmad Abu Bakar said the incident occurred around 8 pm after a patrol team from the Serdang district police headquarters (IPD) Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department ordered a suspicious black Honda City to stop. He said checks on the suspects’ vehicle found two local men, aged 23 and 43, and a 41-year-old woman, who was the driver.

items.title: Entertainment events must respect values and religious sensitivities, says Umno president
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — All programmes, including entertainment events, must be organised with due regard for values, decorum, religious sensitivities and local societal norms, Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. The Deputy Prime Minister said the views expressed by the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (JAWI) and the Federal Territories Mufti Department should be taken seriously and respected, as they reflect the concerns of a broad segment of Malaysian society. Ahmad Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, said guidelines should be reviewed and strengthened to ensure all events remain in line with the nation’s cultural values, religious principles and identity.

items.title: More than half of SPM graduates now opting for TVET, Zahid says as he calls for skills training to be elevated
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the growing number of SPM graduates opting for TVET shows the vocational route is gaining prominence and should be recognised as a central part of the country’s education landscape. He said it is time for TVET to be positioned as a mainstream education route that carries equal standing with academic pathways. “Data also shows that more than half of SPM graduates are now choosing the TVET pathway.

items.title: Zahid: Islamic and character education must be part of mainstream system, National Tahfiz Council proposal headed to Cabinet
items.summary: He said the party backs the creation of a National Tahfiz Council to coordinate and elevate Islamic and tahfiz education nationwide, adding that he will take the proposal to the Cabinet for approval. He then said this ambition should be embedded within a structured national education agenda. Knowledge without ethics creates emptiness,” he said.

items.title: Zahid: Umno wings’ no‑confidence remarks on Negeri Sembilan MB are personal views, not the party position
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has said that statements of no confidence issued by three Umno wings against Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun should be regarded as individual opinions. Earlier today, Negeri Sembilan Umno’s Wanita, Youth and Puteri voiced concern over the relocation of personnel from the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong and the directive to vacate its official residence, describing the move as insensitive given an ongoing customary dispute. “It is clear that this directive was issued while the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong was managing an unresolved dispute involving YTM Datuk Klana Petra Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.

items.title: Crowds swell to 30,000 for Chithirai Pournami at Teluk Intan’s 150‑year‑old temple
items.summary: TELUK INTAN, May 1 — The annual Chithirai Pournami festival here remains a vibrant affair, with some 30,000 Hindu devotees flocking to the Sri Thendayuthapani Temple in Jalan Bandar. Chithirai Pournami refers to the full moon that falls in the first month of the Tamil calendar, known as Chithirai (April-May). Meanwhile, Perak Human Resources, Health, Indian Community Affairs, and National Integration Committee chairman Datuk A. Sivanesan said traders involved in the Chithirai Pournami festival here will be relocated to a new site starting next year.

items.title: Religious affairs minister urges mosques to hold special prayers for abducted Gaza aid flotilla activists
items.summary: BANGI, May 1 — All mosques and surau nationwide have been urged to perform solat hajat and recite Qunut Nazilah for the safety of 175 Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 activists, including 10 Malaysians, who were abducted by the Zionist regime in international waters. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), Dr Zulkifli Hasan, said the government fully supports the GSF 2.0 mission and is closely monitoring developments through the Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC). Zulkifli said the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), together with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, remains consistent in its full support for the GSF 2.0 mission and efforts to liberate Gaza from the Zionist regime.

items.title: Get your umbrellas ready: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning for all states except Terengganu until 9pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning for thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in 12 states, as well as the federal territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, until 9pm today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said the warning covers the entire states of Perlis, Penang, Perak, Selangor and Melaka. Similar weather conditions are expected to hit Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu), as well as Kelantan (Jeli, Tanah Merah, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang).

items.title: Kelantan police: Woman stabbed 61 times before body dumped in Ketereh paddy field
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 1 — A woman’s body with 61 stab wounds was found in a paddy field in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, early this morning. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said police received a call from the public regarding the discovery of the body at about 12.30 am. Commenting on the victim’s condition, he said no identification documents were found.

items.title: Rafizi says summoned by MACC over Arm Holdings JV probe, expects possible charges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Pandan MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has been called up by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to give his statement over an ongoing probe linked to the government’s joint venture with Arm Holdings. In a statement posted on social media this afternoon, Rafizi said he received the notice around 3pm today, requiring him to appear at the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya on May 4 at 10am. No money is involved,” he said.

items.title: Khairy says nostalgia, stability fuel rising youth interest in Umno
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Umno returnee Khairy Jamaluddin said more young Malaysians are now showing interest in joining Umno, attributing the trend to the party’s strong historical track record and perceived stability. “There is also a sense of nostalgia among some younger individuals who grew up during Umno’s period of political dominance,” he told reporters at Umno education congress here today. “When we field young candidates, it shows that Umno is not simply an old party.

items.title: King praises workers’ role in nation’s growth, urges continued focus on welfare
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The role of workers is crucial in driving economic growth and in ensuring the stability and prosperity of the country are maintained, even in the face of increasingly challenging economic conditions, said His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim’s Facebook today, His Majesty said that the welfare of workers must always be given attention, including aspects of safety, work-life balance and career development opportunities, so that they remain resilient in facing economic uncertainties. Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, also conveyed Workers’ Day wishes to all workers who are the backbone of the nation’s development.

items.title: Penang schools battered by storm, officials pledge swift repairs and safety for students
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, May 1 — Four schools in Seberang Perai Utara (SPU), including a primary school, were damaged by a storm late yesterday evening. Penang Education director Mohamad Dziauddin Mat Saad said the schools involved were Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Datuk Haji Abdul Kadir and Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Hashim Awang in Kepala Batas, as well as SMK Gemilang and SMK Permatang Tok Jaya in Butterworth. He said the worst-hit school was SMK Datuk Haji Abdul Kadir, where the roof of a block housing five classrooms was torn off by strong winds, causing structural damage to the first-floor section of the building.

items.title: Defence minister defends high‑achieving SPM student, calls for end to online criticism
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 1 — Kota Tinggi Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin has called on the public to cease all forms of criticism and speculation against a high-achieving Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) student, following the circulation of a video on social media recently. He said the footage showed a casual interaction between him and students during an event in the Kota Tinggi parliamentary and Sedili state constituencies honouring prospective haj pilgrims and 2025 SPM high-achieving students. According to him, the interaction was misinterpreted by certain parties, leading to various negative reactions, including criticism directed at the student.

items.title: Safety of 175 Gaza flotilla activists, including 10 Malaysians, still unconfirmed 38 hours after Israeli detention
items.summary: BANGI, May 1 — The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) has yet to receive any official confirmation on the safety status of 175 Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 activists, including 10 Malaysian participants, after being detained by Israeli authorities in international waters 38 hours ago. SNCC director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said while there was initial information that all the activists would be released at Lerapetra Port, Crete, Greece at noon today (Malaysia time), it has yet to be verified. Sani Araby said they have also been in contact with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim who has stated his firm stand and condemned the Israeli regime for violating international laws.

items.title: Prime Minister Anwar calls for restraint, stresses economy over politics in Negeri Sembilan crisis
items.summary: RAWANG, May 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged all parties to remain calm in addressing the situation in Negeri Sembilan and to prioritise efforts to revive the economy and safeguard the interests of the people. He said the situation in the state must be handled in accordance with the provisions of the state constitution, while respecting the decree of the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun to continue in office. On Monday, all 14 Umno and Barisan Nasional assemblymen in Negeri Sembilan announced they had lost confidence in and withdrawn support from the state government led by Aminuddin.

items.title: Foreign woman dies after falling from 13th‑floor apartment during police narcotics raid in Nilai
items.summary: NILAI, May 1 — A foreign woman in her 20s died after she was believed to have fallen from the 13th floor of an apartment building in Bandar Baru Nilai here at 1.51am today. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the Nilai district police headquarters confirmed receiving information about the death that happened while a police team was conducting a narcotics raid at the apartment building. He advised the public to not speculate on the matter as it could disrupt police investigations and urged those with information on the death to assist investigations by contacting the Nilai district police headquarters or the nearest police station.

items.title: Heavy rain and strong winds forecast nationwide, MetMalaysia issues sweeping alert until evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit nearly all states across the country until 6.00pm this evening. In Perak, the areas involved are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim. The same warning has also been issued for Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak and Petaling in Selangor, as well as the entire Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Blossom Wong, feared Special Branch undercover officer, dies at 87
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Blossom Wong, one of Malaysia’s best-known Special Branch officers and undercover operatives, has died at the age of 87, New Straits Times reported. According to New Straits Times , Wong was regarded as a quiet but formidable figure in the police force. Dr Christina told New Straits Times that her mother’s service in the force never affected their bond.

items.title: Workers welcome post-maternity leave allowance, say it eases burden on new mothers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The introduction of the Post-Maternity Leave Allowance, announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, reflects the government’s concern for working mothers in addressing the rising cost of living. Most public and private sector employees interviewed by Bernama said the additional one-month assistance would allow mothers who have just given birth to undergo their confinement period with greater peace of mind, without financial pressure. Senior assistant director at the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma), Nurfarehin Abd Aziz, 35, said the initiative reflects the government’s commitment to empowering women.

items.title: From Bedrock to Johor: Sultan Ibrahim meets grandson of ‘Flintstones’ co‑creator
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 1 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today received a visit from Dave Williams, the grandson of William Hanna, co-creator of The Flintstones , The Jetsons and numerous other cartoon characters. According to a post on the Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, the meeting took place at Istana Pasir Pelangi here. Williams, who is an economic counsellor at the United States Embassy in Malaysia, also visited Istana Flintstones in Mersing yesterday.

items.title: Food security drive gains RM559.85m boost as Hajiji ties Kaamatan spirit to harvest resilience
items.summary: PAPAR, May 1 — The Sabah state government has allocated a total of RM559.85 million to the state’s Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry (MAFFI) this year to improve food security and the self-sufficiency rate (SSR). Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said various efforts have also been and are being implemented by the government to ensure food security in the state, including the development of padi fields throughout the state, including in Papar and surrounding districts such as Membakut, Beaufort and Kuala Penyu. “In line with the spirit of the Kaamatan Festival which values the harvest, the agriculture sector remains one of the three main pillars that drive the state’s economic growth,” he said at the launching of Sabah state-level Kaamatan Festival here today.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan Umno wings push back, call state’s Balai Undang Luak directive unfair and heavy‑handed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The Negeri Sembilan Umno Wanita, Youth and Puteri wings have expressed concern over the relocation of staff from the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong and the vacating of its official residence, describing the move as insensitive in light of an ongoing customary dispute. In a joint statement, the three wings said they took a “serious view” of the directive issued to relocate all residents at the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong, followed by an instruction to vacate both the official residence and the premises on April 29, 2026. “It is clear that this directive was issued while the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong was managing an unresolved dispute involving YTM Datuk Klana Petra Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.

items.title: Anwar says no more waiting, workers’ initiatives must hit ministers’ desks in two weeks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has given all relevant agencies a two-week deadline to submit reports on the implementation of each workers’ initiative announced today. Today, the announcement is made; within two weeks the report must be on the minister’s desk, and must be presented for Cabinet consideration within three weeks,” he said. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Human Resource Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Cabinet members, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, senior government officials, as well as representatives of workers’ unions and employers.
